questions.prompt: Here a = ⟨2, 1⟩ and b = ⟨1, 2⟩ both start at the origin. Align them head-to-tail, draw a + b, then enter what a + b is.
questions.hint: Once aligned, look only at the a + b arrow. From the origin, count how far it goes right or left (the first number) and how far up or down (the second number).
questions.explanation: Connect a and b head-to-tail, then a + b is the arrow from the start to the end of the path. Add the matching components: ⟨2, 1⟩ + ⟨1, 2⟩ = ⟨2 + 1, 1 + 2⟩ = ⟨3, 3⟩.

questions.prompt: Now try it on your own! Find a + b. Work it out on the grid if it helps.
questions.hint: Look at the a + b arrow from the origin: count how far it goes right or left (the first number) and how far up or down (the second number).
questions.explanation: Connect a and b head-to-tail, then a + b is the arrow from the start to the end of the path. Add the matching components: ⟨−3, 2⟩ + ⟨2, 1⟩ = ⟨−3 + 2, 2 + 1⟩ = ⟨−1, 3⟩.

questions.prompt: Find a + b. Work it out on the grid if it helps.
questions.hint: Look at the a + b arrow from the origin: count how far it goes right or left (the first number) and how far up or down (the second number).
questions.explanation: Connect a and b head-to-tail, then a + b is the arrow from the start to the end of the path. Add the matching components: ⟨2, −3⟩ + ⟨−1, 4⟩ = ⟨2 + (−1), −3 + 4⟩ = ⟨1, 1⟩.

questions.prompt: Find a + b. Work it out on the grid if it helps.
questions.hint: Look at the a + b arrow from the origin: count how far it goes right or left (the first number) and how far up or down (the second number).
questions.explanation: Connect a and b head-to-tail, then a + b is the arrow from the start to the end of the path. Add the matching components: ⟨3, 2⟩ + ⟨−2, −1⟩ = ⟨3 + (−2), 2 + (−1)⟩ = ⟨1, 1⟩.

questions.prompt: Find a + b. Work it out on the grid if it helps.
questions.hint: Look at the a + b arrow from the origin: count how far it goes right or left (the first number) and how far up or down (the second number).
questions.explanation: Connect a and b head-to-tail, then a + b is the arrow from the start to the end of the path. Add the matching components: ⟨−2, 3⟩ + ⟨3, −1⟩ = ⟨−2 + 3, 3 + (−1)⟩ = ⟨1, 2⟩.
